description: LIPITOR is a statin medicine used to lower cholesterol.
indications_and_usage: LIPITOR is indicated to reduce elevated total-C, LDL-C, Apo B, and triglycerides.
dosage_and_administration: The recommended starting dose is 10 or 20 mg once daily.
dosage_forms_and_strengths: Tablets: 10 mg, 20 mg, 40 mg, and 80 mg.
information_for_patients: Patients should be advised to read the patient information leaflet.
adverse_reactions: Most common adverse reactions are nasopharyngitis, arthralgia, diarrhea.
warnings_and_cautions: Skeletal muscle effects and liver enzyme abnormalities may occur.
drug_interactions: Strong CYP3A4 inhibitors increase atorvastatin exposure.
pregnancy: Discontinue as soon as pregnancy is recognized.
pediatric_use: Safety and effectiveness have been established in pediatric patients 10 to 17 years.
geriatric_use: No overall differences in safety or effectiveness were observed.
overdosage: There is no specific treatment for atorvastatin overdose.
storage_and_handling: Store at controlled room temperature 20 to 25°C (68 to 77°F).
how_supplied: Supplied in bottles of 30 and 90 tablets.
clinical_pharmacology: Atorvastatin is a selective inhibitor of HMG-CoA reductase.
mechanism_of_action: Inhibits conversion of HMG-CoA to mevalonate.
pharmacokinetics: Peak plasma concentrations occur within 1 to 2 hours.
